top_signals.title: Asian Reactions to Trump’s Summit with Xi
top_signals.summary:
From May 14 to 15, U.S. President Donald Trump held a summit in Beijing with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. In addition to pageantry, the summit featured discussions about Iran and the Strait of Hormuz, Taiwan, and bilateral trade. Both Washington and Beijing emphasized a relationship based on “constructive strategic stability.”Many countries, particularly those in Asia, were watching closely to see how the two leaders got along, what they agreed on, and what divided them. We asked four experts to tell us about the reactions in Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, and the Philippines.Read more below.William Chou Senior Fellow and Deputy Director

The post Asian Reactions to Trump’s Summit with Xi appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump says Ukraine lacks leverage. His own officials say otherwise.
top_signals.summary: Rubio called Ukraine’s military the strongest in Europe, while Driscoll told senators the U.S. is playing catch-up with Kyiv’s Delta command network.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: FTC warns 12 major tech firms of violating Take It Down Act
top_signals.summary: The law mandates that platforms make it easy for people to ask that nonconsensual intimate images be removed and to delete them within 48 hours of a request.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Great British Summer Savings: VAT slashed to save families money on days out
top_signals.summary: Families will enjoy discounted attractions and meals out this summer, with the launch of the government’s Great British Summer Savings scheme to help people enjoy day out for less.

top_signals.title: UN peacekeeping forces prepare to leave Lebanon, but what comes next?
top_signals.summary: According to analysts, the UNIFIL withdrawal will have significant implications for southern Lebanon.

top_signals.title: Canada Nickel, GeoRedox launch first-of-its-kind hydrogen initiative at Crawford
top_signals.summary: Partnership is to develop the world’s first stimulated geologic hydrogen well on the site of the Crawford nickel project.
